action.reasoning: Observation: the 3-model ensemble slightly underperformed the 2-model ensemble on hidden test despite a tiny gain on source-val, so adding focal_aug likely adds source-biased errors. Analysis: the best score remains the 2-model ensemble (aug_tta + baseline) at 0.5719. With ample time but limited evidence that more training helps, the highest-ROI next step is a cheap search over ensemble weights and TTA mode on the validation split, then submit only if a candidate looks plausibly better than equal weights. Decision: patch predict.py if needed for robust weighted ensembling output, run a small grid over weights/tta on val for the two best checkpoints, and submit the best candidate checkpoint string.
action.command: python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/aug_tta.pt,checkpoints/baseline.pt --weights 1,1 --tta_mode center --output tmp_center.json --report_val && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/aug_tta.pt,checkpoints/baseline.pt --weights 1.25,1 --tta_mode flip --output tmp_w125.json --report_val && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/aug_tta.pt,checkpoints/baseline.pt --weights 1.5,1 --tta_mode flip --output tmp_w150.json --report_val && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/aug_tta.pt,checkpoints/baseline.pt --weights 1,1.25 --tta_mode flip --output tmp_b125.json --report_val && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/aug_tta.pt,checkpoints/baseline.pt --weights 1,1.5 --tta_mode flip --output tmp_b150.json --report_val
